title: Executive Order 10938—Establishing the President's Foreign Intelligence Advisory Board

flags.justification: Members are appointed 'from among qualified persons outside the Government,' and the Board's function is purely advisory — no operational authority is granted.
A clear, structured reporting requirement: 'continuing review and assessment... report thereon to the President each six months or more frequently.'
Single purpose: foreign intelligence oversight and advice.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing oversight function for an ongoing government need (foreign intelligence oversight), evergreen by nature rather than tied to a resolvable emergency.
This creates a new oversight and review mechanism over the CIA and other intelligence agencies — the opposite of removing oversight.

The Board's independent, non-government membership combined with mandatory information access from the agencies it reviews ('shall make available to the Board any information... which the Board may require') constitutes a genuine external check.

Establishes a durable, independent oversight institution for the entire U.S. intelligence community with a broad, ongoing review mandate ('continuing review and assessment of all functions of the Central Intelligence Agency, and of other executive departments and agencies') — a significant, still-existing institutional structure (in modified form) rather than a narrow one-off body.
Sec. 4's revocation of the prior EO 10656 is a clean succession.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/16 = 6.25%. The President's Foreign Intelligence Advisory Board has persisted, with modifications, into the present day — a rare case in this corpus of a body whose textually-claimed ongoing mandate (Flag 9) matches its actual long-term institutional durability.
